tools.name: bash syntax check
tools.run_command: bash -n run.sh && bash -n setup.sh && bash -n util && bash -n operator-tests/common

linting.notes: No linting is configured in the repository. Shellcheck can be used to check bash style and find issues. Validation shows style warnings (legacy backticks, missing quotes) but no critical errors. All scripts pass bash syntax validation.

env_vars: TEST_DIR - points to operator-tests subdirectory (set by run.sh)
MY_SCRIPT - name of currently running test script (set by common)
RESOURCE_DIR - path for temporary files (set by common)
OCP - indicates OpenShift login status (set by util)
testing.notes: This repo is a test framework, not a project with tests. Tests are cloned from external git repos specified in operator-list. The framework requires an active OpenShift cluster login (oc CLI) to run. Without cluster access, only git repository cloning works. The test submodule (openshift-test-kit) must be initialized before running tests. Individual test repos contain .sh files that are executed in alphabetical order.

ci.notes: No CI/CD configuration found. No GitHub Actions, Tekton, Jenkins, or other CI workflows exist in this repository.

build.notes: No build process required. This is a pure bash script framework. The only setup needed is initializing the git submodule for the test library (openshift-test-kit).

conventions.test_naming: Test files are .sh bash scripts in subdirectories under operator-tests/. Files are executed in alphabetical order. All test scripts should source $TEST_DIR/common at the top.
conventions.import_style: Scripts use 'source' to load common libraries and utilities. Common pattern: source $TEST_DIR/common
conventions.notes: Test subdirectories are named after operators and created/populated by setup.sh. Each subdirectory can contain multiple .sh test files. Tests use bash test functions from openshift-test-kit library (os::test::* functions).

lint_commands.command: bash -n run.sh && bash -n setup.sh && bash -n util && bash -n operator-tests/common

lint_commands.notes: Found style issues (SC2006 legacy backticks, SC2086 missing quotes, SC2181 $? usage) but no critical errors

test_commands.notes: Cannot run - requires OpenShift cluster with 'oc' CLI and active login. Requires git submodule initialization and external test repositories.

container_recipe.notes: This repository is a test framework for OpenShift operators, not a testable project. It requires: (1) OpenShift cluster access, (2) oc CLI installed, (3) Active cluster login, (4) Initialized git submodule, (5) Cloned test repositories. Without these dependencies, only syntax/style validation is possible. The framework itself has no unit tests.

results.notes: All bash scripts have valid syntax

results.output_snippet:
SC2046: Quote this to prevent word splitting
SC2006: Use $(...) notation instead of legacy backticks
SC2086: Double quote to prevent globbing and word splitting
SC1091: Not following source files
SC2004: $/${} is unnecessary on arithmetic variables
results.notes: Shellcheck found style/info issues but no critical errors

results.output_snippet:
SC2006: Use $(...) notation instead of legacy backticks
SC2086: Double quote to prevent globbing and word splitting
SC2181: Check exit code directly with e.g. 'if mycmd;'
results.notes: Shellcheck found style/info issues but no critical errors

results.output_snippet:
SC2181: Check exit code with if ! mycmd; not $?
SC2086: Double quote to prevent globbing
SC2046: Quote to prevent word splitting
SC2002: Useless cat
SC2018: Use [:lower:] for portability
results.notes: Shellcheck found style/info issues but no critical errors
Cannot run tests - requires OpenShift cluster, oc CLI, cluster login, initialized submodule, and cloned test repositories. This is a test framework, not a testable project.

validation.summary: Syntax validation OK, shellcheck found style issues (warnings/info only), tests cannot run without OpenShift cluster infrastructure

gaps: No CI/CD configuration - no automated testing on pull requests
No linting configured - shellcheck should be added to CI
No unit tests for the framework itself - only runs external tests
Requires OpenShift cluster access to run - cannot be validated in standard containers
No documentation of required OpenShift permissions or cluster setup
No version pinning for operators or test repositories
Shellcheck issues should be fixed: legacy backticks, missing quotes, direct exit code checks
